old_claim.note: Herbal Medicine Preparation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No herbal-preparation dependency remains because the museum catalog does not assign a medical use, and an application cannot be presumed to cause the artifact form.

source_shape.source_locator: The collection entry identifies the object only as a mortar and pestle and gives no medicinal, botanical, food, or pigment function.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The catalog establishes the paired artifact while leaving its historical application unspecified.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Assigning herbal medicine would turn one possible use into an unsupported causal predecessor and would reverse the likely tool-to-application direction.

ontology_before: A possible later application was treated as the enabling cause of an otherwise use-neutral artifact.
ontology_after: The artifact remains function-neutral until direct residue, context, or textual evidence identifies its use.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: mortar_and_pestle no longer directly depends on herbal_medicine_preparation.
would_reject_if: Residue analysis or a secure archaeological context identified this catalogued pair as equipment made specifically for herbal remedy preparation.
short_reason: The artifact record does not support a medicinal function or reverse application edge.
